toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key frames are from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these, I would need the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with that specific Build ID. The .NET runtime publishes symbol files for its releases. Let me think about how to approach this.

The Build ID is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

Microsoft hosts their symbols on a public symbol server, and I can construct a URL to fetch the symbols for this specific binary using the Build ID in the standard ELF format. I'm working through the correct URL format for the Microsoft symbol server with the build ID I extracted, trying both the binary and debug info paths. Since I might not have dotnet installed, I should attempt a direct download from the symbol server first to retrieve the debug symbols.

arguments.description: Download CoreCLR debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-0624013d2b5c460b8dd35f25c27fe3c8 && BUILD_ID=\"4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801\" && curl -sSL -o libcoreclr.so.dbg \"https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILD_ID}/_.debug\" -w \"%{http_code}\" 2>&1 | tail -1", "description": "Download CoreCLR debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which llvm-symbolizer addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5", "description": "Check available symbolication tools", "initial_wait": 10}
